pullRequest.title: 🎨 Rework information & song properties dialogs
pullRequest.body:
TODOs
-----
* [x] Add _Lyrics_ and _Bookmarks_ tabs to _Edit Tags_, or somewhere else maybe?
* [x] Fix tests
* [ ] Maybe make notebook tabs exist-but-disabled for lyrics and bookmarks of _multiple_ songs?

Changes
-------

### Information
* Move lyrics and bookmarks out of _information_ (these are edit features)
* Remove use of deprecated `Gtk.Table` in favour of `Grid`... or `FlowBox`, in advance of #4057 
* Increase cover art sizes a bit
* Fix a bit of padding and rounded corners.
* Use more modern layout for key-value display
* Better frames for this
* Some smaller modernisation tweaks

### Song Properties aka _Edit_(formerly Edit Tags)
* Move Lyrics and Bookmarks into `SongProperties` Notebook (aka _Edit Tags_, now called _Edit_), but only enable if single song is selected (TODO: maybe disabled tabs?)
* Unify lyrics and bookmark button layouts with existing notebook tabs
* Widen existing margins a little
* Fix lyrics delete buttons

pullRequest.title: Rework and enhance Discord Status Message plugin
pullRequest.body: Check-list ---------- * [X] There is a linked issue discussing the motivations for this feature or bugfix * Fixes #3423 * Fixes #4168 * [X] Unit tests have been added where possible * Created `tests/plugin/test_discord_status.py` in 5ff5beb to test `DiscordStatusMessage.truncate_unicode_text()` - **Required manually adding dependencies to workflows** * [X] I've added / updated documentation for any user-facing features. * Current configuration options for the plugin were not added or changed and still largely reflect current functionality. * [X] Performance seems to be comparable or better than current `main` * No noticeable performance hit in run-time testing What this change is adding / fixing ----------------------------------- This PR reworks the Discord Rich Presence plugin to take advantage of current features/parameters available in pypresence and Discord Rich Presence. Contrasted with the current plugin, there are several notable changes: * When playing music in Quod Libet, rich presence in Discord will show a progress slider that indicates current music progress. * When playing music in Quod Libet, rich presence in Discord displays the "Listening to" prefix (`ActivityType.LISTENING`) to indicate that the user is currently listening to music in the status below the username in the user list. The plugin will use the rich presence details (Status Line 1) to append to the status. * When music is paused in Quod Libet, rich presence in Discord shows the details with a "(Paused)" suffix and removes the progress slider. * When music is stopped (i.e. no song is loaded), the rich presence in Discord will be cleared. This PR also fixes #4168 by truncating characters/graphemes for long detail and state strings that could raise errors when updating rich presence in Discord. With the above fix, this implementation introduces the [`regex`](mrabarnett/mrab-regex) module (`python3-regex` in Debian, Ubuntu) as a dependency of this plugin for grapheme isolation as it supports Unicode grapheme matching that current `re` does not appear to implement. Screenshots -----------

pullRequest.title: 🔧 Unify playlist hidden file check (so be Windows-aware)
pullRequest.body:
* Best we use the same logic as elsewhere I think.
* This involves actually using the hidden file metadata in Windows, not filename
* And on Linux slightly simpler logic for filename detection, even though this code branch probably isn't used much anywhere (XSPF migrations have probably all happened)

Kinda
Closes #3639

* fix regression from 4af0c555
* and add basic tests

Closes #4462

pullRequest.title: Covers: fix / clarify `force_filename` behaviour & migrate to `pathlib`
pullRequest.body:
* If `force_filename` is specified then _really_ force it - no falling back to heuristics (this is also much faster).
* Prefs: Clarify the description & tooltip too, and make the switch tooltip separate from the input box.
* Migrate to `pathlib` - it was _way_ too clunky like this
* ...plus we get `glob`, `.stem`, `.suffix` and a few other things for free
* Tidy some things along the way
* Convert more tests to simple pytest-friendly `assert` statements
* Start adding a bit of typing to `CoverSourcePlugin` world... lots to do though.

Closes #4488

pullRequest.title: Fix/4640 ctl click
pullRequest.body:
It's #4642 thanks to @jdykstra, with linting fixes:

----
During builds for MacOS, patch gdk_event_triggers_context_menu() in gtk+ to recognize control-click as a way to activate pulldown menus. This is documented gtk+ behavior but was broken some time before 3.24.38 (see https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/gtk/-/issues/6724). This patch is their commit 4b5d503c, backported to gtk+ 3.

Update QL to use the GTK Python binding for this API.

Fixes #4640

pullRequest.title: Add SSL support to bundled Python for use by plugins.
pullRequest.body:
Correct build of bundled Python interpreter so that it includes the SSL module.  A bug in the 3.11.1 Python release (and others) caused the "--with-openssl" configure parameter to have no effect when the SSL module was built through setup.py.  Change the module build specification so that it is built through Modules/Setup, which correctly implements the relevant configure parameter.

Fixes #4648.

Check-list
----------

 * [X ] There is a linked issue discussing the motivations for this feature or bugfix
 * [ ] Unit tests have been added where possible
 * [ ] I've added / updated documentation for any user-facing features.
 * [X ] Performance seems to be comparable or better than current `main`


What this change is adding / fixing
-----------------------------------
The immediate cause of this is that the Python interpreter we include in the application bundle isn't being built with SSL support. (MusicBrainz requires https.) The root cause is a deficiency in the Python build scripts. The "--with-openssl" configure parameter is ignored when the "_ssl" module is built by the top-level setup.py script.

pullRequest.title: 🚀 Watch the song library only after UI starts.
pullRequest.body:
Call this explicitly (not in the constructor), later. ...and in GTK idle loop.

Closes #4719

pullRequest.title: Do not try to load GTK `applicationDockMenu_` unless it exists
pullRequest.body:
Check-list
----------

 * [X] There is a linked issue discussing the motivations for this feature or bugfix
 * [ ] Unit tests have been added where possible
 * [ ] I've added / updated documentation for any user-facing features.
 * [X] Performance seems to be comparable or better than current `main`


What this change is adding / fixing
-----------------------------------
This patch fixes the MacOS crash when starting the application. Previously, the app would crash almost immediately due to `applicationDockMenu_` being `None`. This makes sure that it exists and is not an empty variable.

While not the same issue, it was similar to https://github.com/quodlibet/quodlibet/issues/2060, which caused the app to crash after only a few seconds of being open on MacOS.

NOTE: I included a required package for MacOS in this PR, however, it is not required for the patch. I'm happy to remove it if you just want to keep it "patch-only". This just makes it easier to develop on MacOS since it now includes all required packages in Poetry.

Fixes https://github.com/quodlibet/quodlibet/issues/4761

pullRequest.title: 🆙 Rework the Soundcloud API clients
pullRequest.body:
* Use the "new" (undeprecated) auth endpoints
* Choose HLS streams where possible
* When getting stream URLs, we now have to query them first (authenticated) to get to the real CDN content...
* ...which for HLS is actually an `m3u8`, which we have to save locally and pass off to gstreamer as it doesn't support extra headers (auth, here).
* Turns out we can use a HEAD when dealing with legacy (HTTP MP3) streams - that works nicely and saves an entire download (first implementation).
* Add / improve a bunch of typing, for sanity
* Rename "data" to "context" in lots of callback places, much clearer IMO
* Don't rely on `user_favorite`, it's now pointless
* Pass through
* Improve song downloading, to skip non-HTTP things now that these are real (HLS not downloadable easily)
* This all seems to work now, for me at least.

Fixes #4822

Fixes #4821
